steps.title: Dilation preserves angles
steps.content:
When a triangle is dilated (stretched or shrunk), all angles remain unchanged.

This is a fundamental property of similarity and dilation. Since triangle $ABC$ is dilated by a scale factor of 3 to create triangle $XYZ$, each angle in $XYZ$ equals the corresponding angle in $ABC$.

Therefore, $\angle X = \angle A$.

steps.title: Find angle A from the original triangle
steps.content: From the figure of triangle $ABC$, $\angle A = 60^{\circ}$. Since dilation preserves angles, $\angle X = \angle A = 60^{\circ}$. That confirms C is correct.
